userText: I met a strong resistance from conducting survey for student learning effect.
correctedText: I met with strong resistance when conducting a survey on student learning outcomes.

issues.note: 'met resistance' instead of 'met with resistance' — the chunk requires 'with'
issues.suggestion: Drill the full fixed chunk: meet WITH resistance; the 'with' is not optional

issues.note: 'a strong resistance' — resistance is uncountable, no article; also 'conducting survey' missing article → 'a survey'
issues.suggestion: Uncountable nouns: resistance, information, advice, feedback — never use 'a' before them

issues.note: 'from conducting survey' — 'from' in 'meet with resistance from' takes a person/group (from users, from developers), not an activity; use 'when conducting' or 'while conducting'
issues.suggestion: Remember: resistance from [PEOPLE], not resistance from [ACTIVITY]; for activity context use 'when/while + -ing'

issues.note: 'student learning effect' is unnatural — say 'student learning outcomes' or 'the effectiveness of student learning'
issues.suggestion: Use 'learning outcomes' as a fixed chunk in academic/education contexts
